title: The Five Dysfunctions of a Team
author: From: The Five Dysfunctions of a Team
content: Trust is the foundation. Without it, you get fear of conflict, lack of commitment, avoidance of accountability, and inattention to results.
action: Identify the 'weak link' in your team's collaboration.

title: Psychological Safety
author: From: The Fearless Organization
content: High-performing teams require an environment where people feel safe to take risks and be vulnerable in front of each other.
action: Ask a teammate for feedback on a recent mistake.

title: The Law of the Lid
author: From: The 21 Irrefutable Laws of Leadership
content: Your leadership ability determines your effectiveness. You cannot exceed the 'lid' of your own leadership capacity.
action: Study one leadership skill you currently lack.

title: Empowerment via Delegation
author: From: Extreme Ownership
content: Delegation is not abdication. It requires clear intent, defined boundaries, and support.
action: Delegate a recurring task that no longer requires your expertise.

title: The Feedback Gap
author: From: Radical Candor
content: Most people don't get enough constructive feedback because colleagues want to be 'nice.' Care enough to be honest.
action: Ask a peer for one specific change for your latest project.
